kpi_window_note: All % changes in this row are approximately one-month (~30 calendar days). Total market cap from CoinPaprika; BTC dominance and stablecoin share from the top-50 list (CoinGecko, CoinCap fallback).

story_callout.title: How to read this snapshot
bullets: KPI strip: Total market cap and its 1M % come from CoinPaprika. BTC dominance compares Bitcoin’s market cap to that total, with an approximate 1M % using the same total-cap window and BTC’s 30d cap change from this list. Stablecoin share is stablecoin cap vs this top-50 list, with an approximate 1M % from row-level 30d cap changes.
Chart: TradingView TOTAL tracks a broad crypto market-cap index (about the top 125 coins). Use it for trend context; its level can differ from the KPI total because sources and universes differ.
Table: Spot prices, 1M % changes, and categories use the top-50 CoinGecko list (CoinCap fallback when 30-day change is missing). Hover tickers for short About summaries.

top_movers.title: Top movers (1M)
top_movers.footnote: Largest 1-month % moves in the top-50 table (stablecoins excluded). Context lines match recent headlines from dashboard news feeds, with Google News as fallback.

context.title: Bitcoin Slips Under $73K as Crypto Liquidations Near $1B
Ukrainian Police Officers Allegedly Kidnapped Crypto Entrepreneurs to Extort Millions
Pump & Memes HEATING up! XMR vs ZEC! How important are these rate cuts? - Under Exposed

source_note: Total market cap and its 1M change come from CoinPaprika global market data and 30-day market-overview history. BTC dominance uses Bitcoin’s market cap vs that CoinPaprika total; its 1M % approximates how that ratio moved using the same total-cap window and BTC’s 30d change from this list. Stablecoin share is stablecoin market cap vs the sum of this top-50 list; its 1M % uses row-level 30d cap changes (approximate). Coin price changes use CoinGecko spot data with CoinCap fallback for rows that do not include a 30-day change.
key_observations_html:
• Top-50 alts showed mixed 1M moves: 16 names up more than ~0.5%, 18 down more than ~0.5%, and 16 roughly flat out of 50 non-stable coins with a valid 1M % (top-50 table).
  • Others (top-50 coins outside Layer 1, stablecoins, CEX, DeFi, meme, and RWA tabs, e.g. HYPE, MNT, WLFI, ASTER, RLUSD) outpaced Layer 1s over 1M: the stronger group averaged about +44.0% cap-weighted in the top-50, while the weaker averaged about -3.1%—the widest gap among category tabs.
  • Industry headlines emphasize Crypto regulation Recent coverage includes Clarity Act Risks Regulation Without Oversight, Brookings Fellow Says (CoinDesk); Paxos wins SEC approval to clear U.S. stocks on blockchain (CoinDesk). Enforcement and legislation headlines can shift risk appetite across the top-50—watch whether beta concentrates in majors or spreads into higher-beta categories.
  • Industry headlines emphasize Crypto market structure Recent coverage includes Live markets: Bitcoin shrugs off early decline, but two-month winning streak is in jeopardy (CoinDesk); Bitcoin underperforms risk assets as record 9th day of ETF outflows signal waning demand (CoinDesk). Breadth and dominance narratives help explain whether moves are Bitcoin-led or alt-led—compare with BTC/ETH 1M % and the category mix in the top-50 table.

Context only—not investment advice. Observations rank the top-50 table and KPI math on this page against recent crypto and ETF headlines. Bullets are ranked from on-page data and recent industry headlines (dashboard RSS plus Google News fallback).

Key observations are AI generated and should be reviewed for accuracy.
